name: Sitjar Reservoir Loop
name_original: El Embalse del Sitjar — Circular desde Ribesalbes
description: This difficult 13.4-mile circular hike around the Sitjar Reservoir from Ribesalbes offers scenic views and requires good fitness.
region_intro: Bus station hiking trails around Embalse de Sichar offer access to a landscape defined by the reservoir's crystalline waters and surrounding leafy pine forests. Situated on the Mijares River, the Embalse de Sichar covers 317 hectares and is known for its diverse natural features. The region includes viewpoints such as Mirador del Sichar, providing panoramic views, and paths that traverse areas like the Sierra de las Pedrizas, often featuring traditional dry-stone constructions. Best bus station hiking trails around Embalse de Sichar The most popular bus station hiking route is View From the Cave – Cova del Negre loop from Ribesalbes, a 5.9 miles (9.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 40 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of the landscape around the Cova del Negre. Another top favourite among local hikers is Ribesalbes to Fanzara Route – Old Path to Fanzara loop from Ribesalbes, a moderate 6.1 miles (9.9 km) path. This trail follows an old route, connecting Ribesalbes to Fanzara. Local hikers also love the Tree with Views of Fanzara loop from Ribesalbes, a 6.0 miles (9.6 km) trail leading through varied terrain with views towards Fanzara, often completed in about 2 hours 55 minutes. Hiking around Embalse de Sichar is defined by reservoir views, pine forests, and paths that include traditional dry-stone constructions. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging routes. The routes in Embalse de Sichar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 230 reviews. More than 1400 hikers have used komoot to explore Embalse de Sichar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many bus-accessible hiking trails are available around Embalse de Sichar?
A: There are over 80 hiking routes around Embalse de Sichar that are accessible from bus stations. These include a wide range of difficulties, with 23 easy, 57 moderate, and 6 difficult trails to choose from.

Q: What kind of natural features and landscapes can I expect to see on bus-accessible hikes?
A: The region around Embalse de Sichar is known for its stunning natural beauty. You can expect to see the crystalline waters of the reservoir, surrounded by leafy pine forests. Many trails offer panoramic views, such as from the 'Mirador del Sichar' (sometimes called 'del sofá'), and opportunities to observe abundant wildlife like fish and turtles. The routes often traverse diverse landscapes, including glimpses of the Sierra de las Pedrizas.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly hiking options accessible by bus?
A: Yes, there are 23 easy routes suitable for families or those looking for a relaxed walk. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31482131" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente del Arquet – View of Ribesalbes loop from Ribesalbes&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 4.6 km hike. The 'Ruta Lineal: Onda - Embalse del Sichar' is also a good option, connecting the town of Onda directly to the reservoir with viewpoints and rest areas.

Q: Can I bring my dog on these bus-accessible trails?
A: Many trails in the Embalse de Sichar area are dog-friendly, allowing you to enjoy the natural surroundings with your canine companion. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near other hikers, and to check specific trail regulations if available. The serene environment and pine forests offer a great experience for both you and your pet.

Q: What are some notable viewpoints or landmarks along the bus-accessible routes?
A: Hikers often seek out the 'Mirador del Sichar' for its breathtaking panoramic views of the reservoir. Additionally, some routes, like the 'Ruta Lineal: Onda - Embalse del Sichar', ascend to Cota 220, offering various viewpoints. You might also encounter historical elements such as dry-stone constructions along certain itineraries, adding a cultural dimension to your hike.

Q: Are there any circular routes that start and end near a bus station?
A: Yes, many of the bus-accessible trails are circular, allowing for a convenient return to your starting point. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31482970" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View From the Cave – Cova del Negre loop from Ribesalbes&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 9.5 km circular hike. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33793601" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribesalbes to Fanzara Route – Old Path to Fanzara loop from Ribesalbes&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 9.8 km loop.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike around Embalse de Sichar using public transport?
A: The Embalse de Sichar region is enjoyable for hiking throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, making them ideal. Summer can be warm, but the leafy pine forests provide shade, and the crystalline waters invite a refreshing swim. Winter hikes are also possible, offering a quieter experience, though checking weather conditions is always advisable.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails around Embalse de Sichar?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Reviewers often praise the serene atmosphere, the stunning views of the reservoir, and the diverse landscapes that make for a rewarding outdoor experience.

Q: Are there any longer or more challenging bus-accessible hikes for experienced hikers?
A: For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, there are 6 difficult routes available. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19443353" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sitjar Dam – Cova del Negre loop from Ribesalbes&lt;/a&gt;, a demanding 20.3 km hike with significant elevation changes, offering a full day's adventure.

Q: Can I find places to eat or relax near the bus stops or trails?
A: The town of Onda, which serves as a gateway to the area and has bus connections, offers various amenities. Additionally, the Embalse de Sichar itself features a recreational area with tables, benches, and barbecue facilities, perfect for a picnic after your hike. While specific cafes directly at bus stops might vary, you'll find options in nearby towns.

Q: What other activities can be done at Embalse de Sichar besides hiking?
A: Beyond hiking, the Embalse de Sichar is perfect for various outdoor activities. Its waters are suitable for kayaking and paddle surfing. The recreational area is ideal for spending a pleasant day immersed in nature, and the serene environment is perfect for relaxation. The nearby Ermitorio del Santísimo Salvador also offers a peaceful natural setting to explore.
tips: Built in the 1960s, the reservoir is one of the most picturesque natural settings in the province of Castellón and offers a sensation of tranquility like few others. It has several beaches and recreational areas with tables and grills and where you can also take a relaxing bath submerging yourself in its crystal clear waters. Remember that fishing and boating are not allowed.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Sitjar Reservoir circular trail?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas aren't detailed, the trail starts and ends near Ribesalbes. It's generally advisable to look for public parking within the town or designated areas close to the trailhead.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families with young children?
faqs.answer: No, this trail is rated as 'difficult' and requires 'very good fitness' and 'sure-footedness'. It's a long route, typically taking almost 6 hours to complete, making it unsuitable for beginners or families with young children.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this circular route?
faqs.answer: The path is described as 'mostly accessible', but the requirement for 'sure-footedness' indicates there may be uneven or challenging sections. Hikers should be prepared for varied surfaces that demand careful footing.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the trail?
faqs.answer: The primary highlight is the Sitjar Reservoir itself, offering very nice views and a sense of peace. The reservoir also features a recreational area equipped with tables, benches, and a barbecue, perfect for a break.

faqs.question: Is the Sitjar Reservoir circular trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The provided information does not explicitly state whether dogs are allowed or if there are specific leash rules. It's always best to check local regulations or signage at the trailhead regarding pets before you go.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time of year to hike this trail is not specified. However, given its length and difficulty, it's generally recommended to avoid the hottest summer months. Spring and autumn often offer more moderate temperatures for long hikes in this region.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this hike?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that a permit or entrance fee is required to hike this circular route around the Sitjar Reservoir.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or places to rest along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Sitjar Reservoir features a recreational area. This spot is equipped with tables, benches, and a barbecue area, providing a pleasant place to rest and enjoy the surroundings.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Camí de Xorva, Camí de la Pedrissa, Camí de la Llidona, Camí Vell de l'Alcora, Camí El Colmenar, Camí Vell d'Onda, Senda de la Pedrissa, Pont de Remi, and Camí de Ribesalbes a Fanzara.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this challenging hike?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's 'difficult' rating and nearly 6-hour duration, you should bring plenty of water, snacks, appropriate hiking footwear for varied terrain, sun protection, and a first-aid kit. A map or GPS device is also recommended.

faqs.question: Can I find food or accommodation in Ribesalbes after the hike?
faqs.answer: Ribesalbes is a local town, and while specific establishments aren't listed, it's a good place to look for cafes, pubs, restaurants, or accommodation options to refuel and rest after completing the challenging circular route.
